{"query":"Suno AI Songkick acquisition live music concert integration announcement year","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1703,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/suno-is-now-the-controller-of-songkick-user-data-and-is-hiring-a-gm-to-wire-it-into-its-ai-music-platform/","title":"Suno is now the ‘controller’ of Songkick user data – and is hiring a GM to wire it into its AI music platform - Music Business Worldwide","content":"The role reports to Suno Chief Music Officer Paul Sinclair, the former General Manager and EVP of Atlantic Records, who joined the AI company in July 2025.
Suno acquired Songkick from WMG in November as part of the settlement and licensing deal that ended the major music company’s copyright lawsuit against the AI music generator.
The Financial Times reported in April that talks between Suno and both Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment had made little progress, with a person involved in the negotiations telling the newspaper: “We have ongoing engagement, but there is no path forward with the current proposal.” The FT also reported that Suno‘s partnership with WMG itself had seen “minimal progress.”
On the same day as the Songkick data transfer emails went out, Believe CEO Denis Ladegaillerie told MBW that Believe and TuneCore are now blocking distribution of tracks made on unlicensed AI platforms — labeling Suno a “pirate studio.”
Suno reported in February that it had reached 2 million paid subscribers and $300 million in annual recurring revenue.
The company, which says over 100 million people have used its platform, closed a $250 million Series C round in November at a $2.45 billion post-money valuation.Music Business Worldwide"},{"url":"https://www.pastemagazine.com/music/songkick/suno-acquires-songkick-data","title":"Suno acquires Songkick data","content":"In an email, the concert discovery platform revealed that all users’ personal data will be transferred to Suno, an AI music company currently embroiled in licensing issues with Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment."},{"url":"https://www.digitalmusicnews.com/2026/05/03/suno-songkick-concert-discovery-ai/","title":"Suno Ambitiously Plans to ‘Reimagine’ Songkick with AI","content":"Accordingly, Suno now has formal control of concert discovery service Songkick—and all the user data that comes with it.
Seeming in the middle of the night, Suno has posted a job listing for a General Manager of Songkick, described as “a massive untapped opportunity to reimagine what live music discovery experiences look like when powered by AI.”
Notably, this presumably also includes information aggregated through years of Songkick’s important Spotify integration."},{"url":"https://ra.co/news/85117","title":"Suno takes control of Songkick data, emails confirm · News ⟋ RA","content":"Suno has assumed control of Songkick user data, in a move that clarifies how it plans to use the platform following its acquisition from Warner Music Group.
Emails sent to users last Thursday, April 30th, confirm that Suno is now the "controller" of Songkick data, including account details, artist preferences, location data and alert settings, per Music Business Worldwide."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/david-geisser-b28b236b_%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F-%3F%3F-%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F-%3F%3F%3F%3F-%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F-activity-7457008841587699712-5EDH","title":"Suno Acquires Songkick User Data for AI Music Platform | David Geisser posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"Forbes interviewed Suno's CEO Mikey Shulman and missed the key points.
In November 2025, Suno bought Songkick, the concert discovery platform, from Warner Music Group.
While Universal Music Group and Sony Music continue to pursue litigation, Suno has already reached a landmark licensing and partnership agreement with Warner Music Group.
The summer of 2026 is expected to bring a significant fair-use ruling that could set the precedent for the entire AI music industry for years to come.
Deezer confirmed in April 2026 that 44% of its daily uploads are AI generated, with 85% of those fully AI streams flagged as fraudulent."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Suno AI Songkick acquisition live music concert integration announcement year","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.digitalmusicnews.com/2026/05/03/suno-songkick-concert-discovery-ai","title":"Suno Ambitiously Plans to ‘Reimagine’ Songkick with AI","content":"# Suno Ambitiously Plans to ‘Reimagine’ Songkick and Concert Discovery with AI. ## After acquiring Songkick from Warner Music Group in November, Suno plans to use the platform’s juicy user data tranche to reimagine concert discovery with AI. Seeming in the middle of the night, Suno has posted a job listing for a General Manager of Songkick, described as “a massive untapped opportunity to reimagine what live music discovery experiences look like when powered by AI.”. #### The listing says the position involves developing and executing “an integration roadmap that connects Songkick’s live music graph with Suno’s artist and creation ecosystem.” The new hire will also “champion a vision for what it means to move a fan from creating music on Suno to driving live experiences on Songkick.” This role will report to Suno Chief Music Officer Paul Sinclair, a former Atlantic Records General Manager and EVP, who joined Suno last summer.","score":0.84858394,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://hype-index.com/suno-moves-to-integrate-songkick-user-data","title":"Suno Moves to Integrate Songkick User Data","content":"# Suno Moves to Integrate Songkick User Data. #### Suno has begun formally absorbing user data from Songkick, bringing a large pool of fan insight under its control following last year’s acquisition from Warner Music Group. It appears to be an early step in a broader plan to integrate Songkick’s infrastructure into Suno’s AI-led ecosystem. A newly posted role for a General Manager of Songkick outlines plans to connect the platform’s live event data with Suno’s creation tools. The listing frames Songkick as a valuable foundation, pointing to its established network of artist and venue data, while positioning AI as the layer that could redefine how fans discover live music. The hire is expected to shape a product vision that links music creation to real-world experiences, effectively turning user activity on Suno into downstream engagement with concerts and events on Songkick. Emails sent to **Songkick** users on Thursday (April 30) confirmed that personal data held by the platform “will be transferred to **Suno**, who will become the controller responsible for that data going forward.”. It also calls on the GM to “champion a vision for what it means to move a fan from creating music on **Suno** to driving live experiences on **Songkick**.”. **Suno** acquired Songkick from WMG in November as part of the settlement and licensing deal that ended the major music company’s copyright lawsuit against the AI music generator.","score":0.71829855,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pastemagazine.com/music/songkick/suno-acquires-songkick-data","title":"Suno acquires Songkick data","content":"## In an email, the concert discovery platform revealed that all users’ personal data will be transferred to Suno, an AI music company currently embroiled in licensing issues with Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment.
